framing: Freelance full-stack developer applying to vague 'founding engineer' role at PropTech startup

jd.title: Founding Engineer
jd.style_note: Vague/culture-first: minimal requirements, founder-speak, no specific tech stack, equity-heavy

requirements.text: You've shipped products that real users depend on — not just side projects
You're comfortable owning the entire stack, from database schema to deployment pipeline
You move fast and make pragmatic trade-offs — you know when good enough is good enough
You've worked at an early-stage startup before, or you've built something from zero on your own
Bonus: you know real estate, property management, or construction tech

responsibilities: Be the first engineering hire — you'll build the v1 product alongside the two co-founders
Own architecture decisions, tech stack selection, and infrastructure setup
Ship features directly to property managers and landlords who are testing the beta
Hire the next 2-3 engineers when we raise Series A

expected_output.summary: Freelance full-stack developer with 6 years of experience building production web applications for startups and small businesses. Built and shipped 15+ client projects using Next.js, PostgreSQL, and Vercel. Created a SaaS tool for property managers (lease tracking + maintenance requests) that reached 200 paying users before acqui-hired by a competitor. Self-taught engineer, no CS degree. Comfortable across the full stack from database design to CI/CD.

experience.title: Freelance Software Engineer
bullets: Built and shipped 15+ production web applications for startup clients using Next.js, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, and Vercel
Created RentTrack — a SaaS lease management tool for small landlords (Next.js, Supabase, Stripe), grew to 200 paying users
Negotiated acqui-hire of RentTrack by competitor in 2024 — integrated codebase and transitioned users over 3-month period
Designed database schemas, built REST APIs, implemented auth (Supabase Auth, NextAuth), and deployed via Vercel and Railway
Managed client relationships, scoped projects, and delivered on fixed-price contracts ($5K-$40K range)

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Built internal tools for property valuation pipeline using React, Node.js, and PostgreSQL
Developed automated comparable property analysis feature processing 50K+ listings daily
Participated in on-call rotation for production systems serving 15K+ monthly home offers

education: Self-taught — freeCodeCamp, Codecademy, and open source contributions (2017-2019)

jd_requirements: shipped products real users depend on
entire stack database schema deployment pipeline
early-stage startup built something from zero
real estate property management construction tech
deliberate_flaws: No CS degree — self-taught (some startups care, most YC companies don't)
Freelancer — no experience on a permanent engineering team (Opendoor was 2 years, then freelance)
No experience hiring engineers (JD says 'hire next 2-3 engineers')
Vague JD means candidate must infer tech requirements — could be wrong stack entirely
RentTrack was small scale (200 users) — founding engineer role will need to scale to thousands
No mobile development experience (property managers often need mobile apps)
Docker experience is basic — production infrastructure knowledge may be shallow beyond Vercel/Railway
Fixed-price freelance model doesn't prepare for startup equity/salary dynamics
